title: Bidar Fort
tagline: Bidar Fort — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Bidar Fort is located in the old city area, Bidar, Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Bidar Fort is located in the old city area, Bidar, Karnataka, India. The fort, the city and the district are all affixed with the name Bidar. Sultan Ahmad Shah I of the Bahmanid dynasty shifted his capital from Gulbarga to Bidar in 1427 and built his fort along with a number of Islamic monuments. There are over 30 monuments inside Bidar fort.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Solah Khamba Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Solah Khamba Mosque is a mosque within the Bidar Fort in Bidar, in the state of Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Mahmud Gawan Madrasa
topPlaces.description: The Mahmud Gawan Madrasa is a former madrasa, now mosque, in partial ruins, located in Bidar, in the state of Karnataka, India. It was completed in 876 AH (1471/1472 CE) and is an example of the regional style of Indo-Islamic architecture under the Bahmani Sultanate.

topPlaces.name: Gajapati invasion of Bidar
topPlaces.description: The Gajapati invasion of Bidar in 1461 was a significant military expedition by Kapilendra Deva of the Gajapati Empire against the Bahmani Sultanate. This event unfolded in the aftermath of Humayun Shah Bahmani's demise and the subsequent ascent of Nizam Shah Bahmani to the throne.

topPlaces.name: Bidar
topPlaces.description: Bidar ( Kannada: [biːd̪əɾ] ) is a city and the headquarters of the Bidar district in Karnataka state of India. Bidar is a prominent place on the archaeological map of India.

topPlaces.name: Chaubara (watchtower)
topPlaces.description: Chaubara, also spelt Choubara, is a historic watchtower located in Bidar, in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is listed as a state protected monument.

topPlaces.name: Kamthana
topPlaces.description: Kamthana is a village in Bidar taluka of Bidar district, in the Indian state of Karnataka. Kamthana's ancient village, Jain Tirtha (pilgrimage), is about 10 km from Bidar, famous for the historical Parshavanth Tirthankara Jain temple.

topPlaces.name: Manhalli
topPlaces.description: Manhalli, or Manalli, is a small town in Bidar taluka of Bidar district in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is located 20 km from Bidar.

topPlaces.name: Bagdal
topPlaces.description: Bagdal is a small town in Bidar taluka of Bidar district in the Indian state of Karnataka.

items.note: The Solah Khamba Mosque is a mosque within the Bidar Fort in Bidar, in the state of Karnataka, India.

The Mahmud Gawan Madrasa is a former madrasa, now mosque, in partial ruins, located in Bidar, in the state of Karnataka, India. It was completed in 876 AH (1471/1472 CE) and is an example of the regional style of Indo-Islamic architecture under the Bahmani Sultanate.

The Gajapati invasion of Bidar in 1461 was a significant military expedition by Kapilendra Deva of the Gajapati Empire against the Bahmani Sultanate. This event unfolded in the aftermath of Humayun Shah Bahmani's demise and the subsequent ascent of Nizam Shah Bahmani to the throne.
Bidar ( Kannada: [biːd̪əɾ] ) is a city and the headquarters of the Bidar district in Karnataka state of India. Bidar is a prominent place on the archaeological map of India.
Chaubara, also spelt Choubara, is a historic watchtower located in Bidar, in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is listed as a state protected monument.

Kamthana is a village in Bidar taluka of Bidar district, in the Indian state of Karnataka. Kamthana's ancient village, Jain Tirtha (pilgrimage), is about 10 km from Bidar, famous for the historical Parshavanth Tirthankara Jain temple.
Manhalli, or Manalli, is a small town in Bidar taluka of Bidar district in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is located 20 km from Bidar.
Bagdal is a small town in Bidar taluka of Bidar district in the Indian state of Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bidar Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bidar Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bidar Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (122 km) and the nearest railway station is Secunderabad Junction (Hyderabad) (117 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bidar Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bidar Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bidar Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Bidar Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bidar Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Solah Khamba Mosque, Mahmud Gawan Madrasa, Gajapati invasion of Bidar, Bidar, Chaubara (watchtower).
